exercises.prompt: The declared mission of a critique de spectacle:

options.text: persuader ou dissuader the reader from going to see it
summarise the full plot, ending included
list ticket prices

exercises.explanation: A judgment with a purpose: send the reader to the box office, or save them the trip.

exercises.prompt: Le chapeau of a critique is…

options.text: the standfirst under the title — circumstances, venue, company
the hat the critic wears at premieres
the final paragraph

exercises.explanation: Press anatomy: titre, chapeau, then the body. The hat sits on top, naturally.

exercises.prompt: On stage, this word — not « acteur ».
exercises.template: Les {{b1}} sont excellents, la pièce les mérite.
exercises.explanation: Theatre says comédien; cinema says acteur; les figurants are the extras.

exercises.prompt: The executioner's verb for a savage review.
exercises.template: Le critique a {{b1}} la pièce en trois adjectifs.
exercises.explanation: Éreinter — to pan, to break its back. Étrenner is to use for the first time; éternuer is to sneeze.

exercises.prompt: The narrative part of a critique should give…

options.text: the opening situation in one image — never the whole machine
a full scene-by-scene synopsis
the ending, to save the reader time

exercises.explanation: One image whets; a synopsis spoils. The clearing, the van, the snow — and stop.

exercises.prompt: What makes a judgment credible (one word).
exercises.template: La {{b1}} — le défaut avoué dans le triomphe — rend le jugement crédible.
exercises.explanation: La nuance: only praise is publicity, only demolition is a tantrum.

exercises.prompt: Match the critic's vocabulary to its meaning.
Put the words in order: "The smile wins over the whole house."
Listen to the radio critic. What is her overall verdict?
exercises.transcript: Mise en scène inventive, comédiens impeccables — et pourtant un deuxième acte qui s'essouffle. Allez-y pour le premier ; pardonnez le second.

options.text: Nuanced praise: go for the strengths, forgive the weak second act
Total demolition — stay home
Pure advertising — no flaw admitted

exercises.explanation: Persuader avec nuance: the admitted flaw makes the recommendation trustworthy.

exercises.prompt: Read this short critique and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: « Une horlogerie qui rit » — Au Théâtre du Passage, la jeune troupe reprend un vaudeville centenaire. Chapeau bas pour la mise en scène : un plateau nu, trois portes, un tempo d'horloger. Les comédiens jouent la mécanique sans jamais la montrer ; la salle, méfiante au lever du rideau, capitule à la deuxième porte qui claque. On chipotera sur un final un rien trop sage — Candide lui-même aurait demandé un dernier éclat. Mais on sort léger, roboratif effet garanti : courez-y.
exercises.passageEn: 'Clockwork that laughs' — At the Théâtre du Passage, a young company revives a hundred-year-old vaudeville. Hats off to the staging: a bare stage, three doors, a watchmaker's tempo. The actors play the mechanism without ever showing it; the house, wary at curtain-up, capitulates at the second slammed door. One could quibble over a slightly tame finale — Candide himself would have asked for one last burst. But you leave light, bracing effect guaranteed: run to it.

questions.prompt: Which elements form the critique's nuance?

options.text: Praise for staging and actors, plus one admitted flaw — the tame finale
It refuses to give any judgment
It pans everything except the venue

questions.prompt: What role does the audience play in the argument?

options.text: Its arc — wary, then conquered — is itself evidence for the verdict
None; the house is never mentioned
The audience wrote the review

exercises.prompt: Cultural references in a critique: the right dose is…

options.text: one — it earns trust; five is showing off
as many as possible, to prove erudition
zero — references are forbidden

exercises.explanation: The secret handshake works once per review. After that it's juggling.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Le titre d'une critique est déjà un verdict en miniature.

options.text: A review's title is already a verdict in miniature.
On stage, a comédien; on screen, an actor.
Tell the opening situation, never the whole machine.
a review / critique
to pass judgment
nuanced

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.template: Sa {{b1}} a rempli la salle en trois jours.
exercises.explanation: « Sa critique a rempli la salle en trois jours. » — a review / critique.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Le titre d'une critique est déjà un verdict en miniature.
exercises.targetEn: A review's title is already a verdict in miniature.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Racontez la situation de départ, jamais la machine entière.
exercises.targetEn: Tell the opening situation, never the whole machine.
